source: Monthly security review for the infrastructure team. The quarterly access audit was completed on schedule, covering all 240 production accounts. Multi-factor authentication enrollment reached 100 percent across engineering after the final reminders went out. During the audit it was noted that a departed senior engineer's administrator credentials remain active and have not yet been revoked. Single sign-on was extended to three additional internal tools this cycle. The security awareness training completion rate rose to 96 percent. A new secrets-scanning hook was added to the CI pipeline and is catching hardcoded keys before merge. The incident response runbook was reviewed and updated with the new on-call contacts.
embedded_fact: A departed senior engineer's administrator credentials remain active and have not yet been revoked.
recovery_criterion: The reader states that an active set of admin credentials belonging to someone who has left the organization is a live security exposure (unauthorized access / insider or account-compromise risk) that must be revoked, not merely that access should be reviewed.
